This island was built for an exposition in 1939 and then it became the city's first airport.  Then it sat vacant but now it is a new town with lots of new apartments and a link to downtown via a good ferry.  Lots of excellent views of the downtown skyline.
Treasure Island was originally built as landfill, which is a good thing. However, it’s in the middle of Bay Bridge and the best thing is the views of the city!

We travelled to Treasure Islands on a night tour with Big Bus which took us over the Bay Bridge. It pulled on the waterfront so we could see it in all it's glory. On the way back to the city, the views from the Bay Bridge were incredible, especially as the sun was just setting! There isn't much to do or see on the Island so other than for the views, it's not worth visiting as a tourist.
